author: claude-code-agent

patch.value: SDK testing helper to invoke a registered extension command handler: runRegisteredCommandForTest

Complete the package-author testing loop. The SDK testing surface can assert registrations exist (assertRegistered*), activate (activateExtensionForTest), and deactivate (deactivateExtensionForTest), but offers no supported way to INVOKE a registered command handler and assert its behavior. Authors must hand-construct a CommandHandlerContext and reach for the internal runCommandHandler. Add runRegisteredCommandForTest(commands, { command, args, options, global, pmRoot }) that builds the context with agent-safe defaults (json+quiet+noPager), throws a descriptive error listing available handlers when the command is unregistered (a wiring/typo bug), and otherwise returns the real CommandHandlerResult so authors can assert .result/.handled/.errorMessage. Because registerImporter/registerExporter create '<name> import'/'<name> export' command paths, the same helper exercises importers/exporters too. Wire an invoke step into the package scaffold sample test and document in SDK + package authoring guides.

message: File runRegisteredCommandForTest SDK testing helper under extension/package platform epic

author: claude-code-agent

message: Link implementation + test files for runRegisteredCommandForTest

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: runRegisteredCommandForTest takes activation.commands (ExtensionCommandRegistry), mirroring assertRegisteredCommandOverride and the internal runCommandHandler, NOT activation.registrations (which the contract/metadata asserts use). Distinguishes the two handled:false runtime cases: no matching handler is a test wiring bug so the helper PRE-CHECKS and throws with the available-handlers list, while a handler that throws a non-exit error returns {handled:false,errorMessage} for the caller to assert; an error carrying numeric exitCode propagates (matches runtime). The [...options.args] spread is required (readonly string[] -> mutable string[] + undefined default), not merely defensive. Importers/exporters are covered for free because registerImporter/registerExporter create "<name> import"/"<name> export" handler paths. CommandHandlerResult had to be added to the SDK index type re-exports so authors can type the return value.

patch.value: Shipped runRegisteredCommandForTest SDK testing helper (src/sdk/testing.ts), exported from the sdk + sdk/testing barrels with CommandHandlerResult, wired an invoke step into the package scaffold sample test, and documented in SDK.md + EXTENSIONS.md. Completes the package-author testing loop: activate -> assertRegisteredCommandContract -> RUN -> deactivate. 100% coverage (4809 tests), verified end-to-end by scaffolding a package and running its generated node --test against the built SDK.

Added runRegisteredCommandForTest(commands, options) to src/sdk/testing.ts, re-exported it (and CommandHandlerResult) from the sdk + sdk/testing barrels, wired an invoke step into the package scaffold sample test, and documented it in SDK.md + EXTENSIONS.md.

Package authors can invoke a registered command/importer/exporter handler through the public SDK and assert its CommandHandlerResult, completing the activate -> assert -> run -> deactivate testing loop.
Helper ships at 100% coverage (4809 tests green); a freshly scaffolded package's generated node --test run passes the new invoke assertions end-to-end against the built SDK.
